purpose: Classifies carry unwind events into three tiers based on credit cycle position at the time of the unwind, enabling conditional severity assessment.
components: Contained tier: tight credit (IG <130bp, HY <400bp) — examples: 2013, 2018, Aug 2024
Amplified tier: turning credit — example: 1998 LTCM
Systemic tier: late-cycle credit — example: 2008 GFC
Current positioning: IG ~110bp, HY ~380bp → toward contained classification
current_assessment: Current credit spreads place the next carry unwind toward contained classification. The 2025-2027 maturity wall introduces time-varying risk that could shift classification over the projection horizon. The hierarchy is the most empirically robust finding in the entire analysis with no agent challenging it.
limitations: Credit spread thresholds between tiers are not precisely defined
The transition from contained to amplified to systemic may not be gradual
Maturity wall creates calendar-dependent risk that complicates static classification
Framework assumes credit cycle is observable in real-time; turning points are often identified only retrospectively
